d: Create component: select frame or group then Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+K, or right-click > Create component. This converts the selection into a main component.
Main component is the source of truth for all its instances. Identified by a purple filled diamond icon in the layers panel. All changes to the main propagate to instances.
Instance is a linked copy of a main component, shown with a hollow diamond icon. Create by dragging from the assets panel or Alt+drag from the main component on canvas.
RULE: Always build components from frames with auto layout, never from groups. Groups do not support auto layout or constraints and lead to brittle components.
RULE: Set up constraints and auto layout on the frame BEFORE converting it to a component. Retrofitting layout after component creation causes issues with existing instances.
Components should be self-contained: include internal padding, background fills, and sizing logic. Do not rely on parent containers for spacing or visual treatment.

Boolean property toggles layer visibility (true/false). Use for show/hide icon, badge, description. Create: right-click layer > Add boolean property, or click the visibility eye icon while editing the component definition.

Text property exposes text content for editing without double-clicking into the instance. Create: select text layer > click content property icon, or right-click > Add text property. Users edit text directly from the properties panel.

Instance swap property allows swapping a nested instance for another component. Create: select nested instance inside component > right-click > Add instance swap property. Set preferred values to limit which components appear in the swap dropdown.
Variant property switches between variants in a component set. Created automatically when you combine variants. Values correspond to the variant names (e.g., Size=Small, Size=Medium, Size=Large).
Variants are multiple versions of a component grouped together in a component set, shown with a purple dashed border. Each variant represents a distinct visual or behavioral state of the component.

Create variants: select a component then click the + button in the variant section of the properties panel, or select multiple separate components and choose Combine as variants to merge them into one component set.

Variant properties are named dimensions like Size (sm/md/lg), State (default/hover/pressed/disabled), Type (primary/secondary/ghost). Layer name format is Property=Value pairs: e.g., Size=Large, State=Hover.
RULE: Plan all variant combinations BEFORE building. Unplanned additions cause variant explosion. Map out every needed combination of properties and values on paper or in a spreadsheet first.
RULE: Use consistent property names across all variants in the set. If one variant uses State=Hover, all variants must have the State property. Inconsistent naming breaks the component set.
Use the Change to interaction in prototyping to switch between variants on hover, press, or other triggers. This enables interactive component prototypes (e.g., button hover state, toggle switch).
A component set is the container holding all variants, displayed with a purple dashed outline. All components within a set MUST share identical property structure (same property names and types).
Right-click a component set > Reorganize variants to auto-arrange all variants into a clean grid layout. Each unique combination of property values equals one variant in the grid.
RULE: Do not create variants for every color. Use variables instead to reduce variant count. Color theming should be handled by variable modes, not by multiplying variants.

RULE: Max recommended variants per component set is approximately 20-30. Beyond that threshold, consider restructuring by splitting into sub-components or using variables and boolean properties to reduce combinations.
Place component instances inside other components for composability. Benefits: reusable micro-components, cascading updates from inner component changes, and clean separation of concerns.

Example: Button component contains an Icon component (instance swap) plus a Text layer (text property). Card component contains Avatar (instance swap) + Title (text property) + Button (nested component instance).

Expose nested properties to parent: click the nested instance inside the main component, right-click on the desired property, then select Expose to parent. This lets users edit nested properties directly from the outer component's properties panel.
RULE: Build atomic components first (icons, badges, labels), then compose them into molecules (buttons, inputs, tags), then assemble molecules into organisms (cards, navbars, forms).

Compound components are built from multiple other components. Examples: Navigation bar = Logo + NavLinks + SearchBar + UserMenu. Form field = Label + Input + HelperText + ErrorMessage with boolean toggles for optional parts.

Each sub-component in a compound component maintains its own properties internally. Expose only the properties that end users actually need at the top level to keep the properties panel clean and usable.

Slots are placeholder containers inside components where designers can add custom content. Add a frame inside the component and mark it as a slot. Designers can then add their own layers inside instance slots without detaching the instance.

Slot constraints specify which component types a slot accepts. Philosophy shift from prevent all deviation to enable creativity within boundaries. Common uses: card content areas, modal bodies, list item content, toolbar action areas.

Overrides are changes made to an instance that differ from its main component. Overridable properties: text content, fill color, stroke, effects, nested instance swaps, and layer visibility. Changed properties show a blue dot indicator.

Properties NOT overridable on instances: layer structure (cannot add or remove layers), auto layout direction, and constraints. To change these, you must edit the main component or detach the instance.

Reset overrides: right-click instance > Reset all overrides, or reset individual properties. Push overrides to main: right-click instance > Push changes to main component. Overrides persist through non-breaking component updates.
Detach instance: right-click > Detach instance (Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+B). Result: instance becomes a regular frame and loses its link to the main component. No further automatic updates from main.

RULE: Avoid detaching instances as it breaks the design system chain. Only detach when creating a brand-new component FROM an existing one, or for truly one-off special layouts that cannot be achieved with overrides or slots.

Use slash hierarchy in component names to create grouping in the assets panel: Button/Primary, Button/Secondary, Button/Ghost. Format: Category/Type/Variant. Example: Input/Text/Default, Input/Select/Open.

RULE: First word is the component category (Button, Input, Card, Modal, Icon). Use PascalCase or Title Case. Keep names concise but descriptive. Icons: Icon/Arrow/Right, Icon/Social/Twitter. Patterns: Pattern/Hero, Pattern/Footer.

Set preferred values on instance swap properties to limit which components appear in the swap dropdown. Set in component property settings > Preferred values > select allowed components or entire component sets. RULE: Always set preferred values for icon instance swaps.

Add description: select component > Design panel > description field. Description appears in assets panel tooltip, instance panel, and dev mode. Supports markdown: **bold**, _italic_, links, lists. Document purpose, when to use, when NOT to use, and required properties.

State management pattern: use a variant property named State for interactive states. Common values: default, hover, active/pressed, focus, disabled. Combine with the Change to prototype interaction for interactive previews.

Responsive component pattern: use variant property for breakpoints (mobile/tablet/desktop) or auto layout with min/max widths. Additional variant states: Skeleton/Loading with placeholder shapes, Empty state with placeholder content, Error state with error styling and message.

Bind variables directly to component properties for automatic theme switching. When the variable mode changes (e.g., light to dark), all bound properties update automatically without needing separate variants per theme.

RULE: One component per concern. Do not mix unrelated elements (e.g., Button and Input) in the same component. Each component should represent a single UI element or a coherent grouping of related elements.

RULE: Use auto layout in ALL components, never fixed positioning for internal layout. Set proper resize behavior (fill container or hug contents) BEFORE converting to component. Test component at multiple sizes before publishing.

RULE: Include padding and spacing inside the component itself, do not rely on external spacing from parent frames. Use variables for all color and spacing values inside components, never hardcode raw hex colors or pixel values.
